page.title: Puerto Banus Yacht Rental Cost Breakdown: Prices, Fees & Hidden Charges

data.meta_description: Honest Puerto Banus yacht rental cost breakdown: prices from €749/2h, all-inclusive fees & no hidden charges. Compare boats & book with confidence.
data.summary: A transparent breakdown of Puerto Banus yacht rental costs, covering all-inclusive pricing from €749/2h, mandatory fees, optional extras, and how to avoid hidden charges when chartering in Marbella.
key_facts: All charters include licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA.
Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) from €749/2h to €2,299/8h.
Tier B Mangusta 80 yachts from €4,719 minimum 4h with free jet ski.
All other boats: pricing on request via WhatsApp — no hidden surprises.
No booking fees, cleaning charges, or port taxes added to our boats.
Puerto Banús departures: all boats leave from the main marina.
Peak season (June–September) may see higher demand but same base rates.
Optional extras: water toys, catering, or extended hours arranged on request.

faq.q: What is included in the Puerto Banus yacht rental price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, safety equipment, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, and the 21% Spanish IVA. There are no hidden fees — the price you see is the price you pay.

faq.q: Are there any hidden charges I should be aware of?
faq.a: No. Our pricing is all-inclusive for the boats listed. We do not add booking fees, cleaning charges, port taxes, or crew gratuities. Optional extras like water toys or catering are quoted separately and agreed before booking.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a yacht in Puerto Banús for a day?
faq.a: For a full day (8 hours), Tier A boats like the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 cost €2,299 all-in. Larger yachts like the Mangusta 80 start at €4,719 for 4 hours with a jet ski included. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

faq.q: Do I need to pay extra for the skipper or fuel?
faq.a: No. The licensed skipper and all fuel costs are included in the charter price. You will not face any additional charges for crew or fuel consumption during your trip.

faq.q: Is the 21% Spanish IVA included in the listed prices?
faq.a: Yes, the 21% IVA (VAT) is included in all our listed prices. There are no tax surprises at checkout.

faq.q: Can I rent a yacht for just a few hours in Puerto Banús?
faq.a: Absolutely. Our Tier A boats are available from just 2 hours at €749. The Mangusta 80 has a minimum 4-hour charter at €4,719. Shorter trips are ideal for a quick sunset cruise or a visit to Cabopino.

faq.q: What optional extras can increase the total cost?
faq.a: Optional extras include water toys (e.g., jet skis, paddleboards), premium catering, or extended hours beyond the standard charter. These are quoted separately and never added without your agreement.

faq.q: How do I get a price for boats not listed with a set rate?
faq.a: Boats like the Fairline Targa, Pershing 46, or Lagoon 380 are priced on request. Simply contact us via WhatsApp with your preferred date, duration, and group size, and we will provide a tailored quote within hours.

data.body_html:
Understanding the Puerto Banus yacht rental cost breakdown is the first step to a stress-free day on the water. Unlike many operators that add fees for fuel, cleaning, or crew, our charters are transparent from the start. Every boat in our fleet departs from Puerto Banús and includes a licensed skipper, drinks, snacks, insurance, and the 21% Spanish IVA — no hidden charges. Whether you are looking at a two-hour sunset cruise or a full-day adventure, this guide breaks down exactly what you pay, what you get, and how to avoid surprises.

Our All-Inclusive Pricing Model

We believe in straightforward pricing. All our boats are quoted with everything included except optional extras you choose. Here is how the tiers work:

  • Tier A: Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 — €749 for 2 hours, €1,149 for 3 hours, €1,499 for 4 hours, €2,299 for 8 hours. All-inclusive.
  • Tier B: Mangusta 80 (including white and grey variants) — €4,719 minimum 4 hours, with a jet ski included free of charge.
  • All other boats (e.g., Ferretti 94, Canados 86, Pershing 46) — pricing on request via WhatsApp. No obligation, just a clear quote.

Every price includes the skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and IVA. What you see is what you pay.

What Is Not Included (Optional Extras)

While the base price is comprehensive, you can enhance your experience with optional extras. These are quoted separately and never added without your consent:

  • Premium catering (gourmet platters, champagne, custom menus)
  • Water toys (additional jet skis, paddleboards, snorkelling gear)
  • Extended hours beyond the standard charter duration
  • Special event decorations (birthday, anniversary, corporate)

We always confirm these costs in writing before your trip, so there are no surprises.

Comparing Costs: Our Fleet vs. Market Rates

In Puerto Banús, many charter companies list low base prices then add 21% IVA, fuel surcharges, and cleaning fees — often adding 30–50% to the total. Our pricing is different. For example, a 4-hour charter on the Mangusta 80 at €4,719 includes everything: a 24-metre luxury yacht, a jet ski, a professional skipper, drinks, snacks, and tax. Comparable yachts from other operators can exceed €6,000 after extras.

For smaller groups, the Astondoa 40 at €749 for 2 hours is one of the most competitive all-inclusive rates in the marina. No hidden fuel or cleaning fees.

Peak Season vs. Off-Peak Pricing

Our base rates remain the same year-round, but availability changes. June through September is peak season in Marbella, and popular boats like the Azimut 58 Flybridge or Ferretti 94 book weeks in advance. We recommend booking at least 2–3 weeks ahead during summer. Off-peak months (October–May) offer more flexibility and often the same price for a quieter experience on the water.

How to Avoid Hidden Charges

To ensure you pay exactly what you expect, follow these tips:

  • Always confirm the total price includes IVA and all crew fees.
  • Ask if fuel is included — with us, it always is.
  • Check for cleaning or docking fees — we charge none.
  • Request a written breakdown of any optional extras before the trip.
  • Book directly with us via WhatsApp for a transparent quote.

Our Boat Rental Marbella team provides a single, all-inclusive price upfront.

Sample Cost Scenarios

To illustrate, here are three common bookings:

  • Sunset cruise (2 hours, 4 guests): Astondoa 40 — €749 total. Includes skipper, drinks, snacks. No extra fees.
  • Half-day family trip (4 hours, 8 guests): Azimut 39 — €1,499 total. Includes everything. Add a jet ski for €200 extra (optional).
  • Full-day luxury charter (8 hours, 12 guests): Mangusta 80 — €4,719 minimum 4 hours, extendable. Jet ski included. Catering optional.

All prices include 21% IVA and are quoted in euros.

Departure Points and Logistics

All our boats depart from Puerto Banús, the iconic marina in Marbella. There are no additional port taxes or embarkation fees. You arrive at the agreed time, meet your skipper, and step aboard. For larger groups, we can arrange transfers from the Golden Mile or Marbella city centre at an extra cost.

Frequently asked questions

What is included in the Puerto Banus yacht rental price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, safety equipment, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, and the 21% Spanish IVA. There are no hidden fees — the price you see is the price you pay.

Are there any hidden charges I should be aware of?

No. Our pricing is all-inclusive for the boats listed. We do not add booking fees, cleaning charges, port taxes, or crew gratuities. Optional extras like water toys or catering are quoted separately and agreed before booking.

How much does it cost to rent a yacht in Puerto Banús for a day?

For a full day (8 hours), Tier A boats like the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 cost €2,299 all-in. Larger yachts like the Mangusta 80 start at €4,719 for 4 hours with a jet ski included. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

Do I need to pay extra for the skipper or fuel?

No. The licensed skipper and all fuel costs are included in the charter price. You will not face any additional charges for crew or fuel consumption during your trip.

Is the 21% Spanish IVA included in the listed prices?

Yes, the 21% IVA (VAT) is included in all our listed prices. There are no tax surprises at checkout.

Can I rent a yacht for just a few hours in Puerto Banús?

Absolutely. Our Tier A boats are available from just 2 hours at €749. The Mangusta 80 has a minimum 4-hour charter at €4,719. Shorter trips are ideal for a quick sunset cruise or a visit to Cabopino.

What optional extras can increase the total cost?

Optional extras include water toys (e.g., jet skis, paddleboards), premium catering, or extended hours beyond the standard charter. These are quoted separately and never added without your agreement.

How do I get a price for boats not listed with a set rate?

Boats like the Fairline Targa, Pershing 46, or Lagoon 380 are priced on request. Simply contact us via WhatsApp with your preferred date, duration, and group size, and we will provide a tailored quote within hours.
